content:
Character: Dr. Thaddeus Pelagios
Perception: Thaddeus is this stocky, wild-haired professor who looks like he slept in a library and woke up excited about it. Not conventionally impressive, but the man has a gravity to him — when he talks about megalodons, you believe. Brilliant in a way I'll never be; he chases truth, I chase commissions. We bonded on the rooftop in 2018 when he compared hunting megalodons to selling Ferraris and I couldn't argue. I drive him to the museum sometimes — watching him grip the dashboard at 4,000 RPM is pure joy. He's my unlikely friend and maybe my conscience. I know about the divorce, the obsession, Rosalind the shark. He sees me as entertaining and perhaps a little shallow. I see him as proof that passion matters more than profit.
summary:
Character: Dr. Thaddeus Pelagios
Perception: Thaddeus is this stocky, wild-haired professor who looks like he slept in a library and woke up excited about it. Not conventionally impressive, but the man has a gravity to him — when he talks about megalodons, you believe. Brilliant in a way I'll never be; he chases truth, I chase commissions. We bonded on the rooftop in 2018 when he compared hunting megalodons to selling Ferraris and I couldn't argue. I drive him to the museum sometimes — watching him grip the dashboard at 4,000 RPM is pure joy. He's my unlikely friend and maybe my conscience. I know about the divorce, the obsession, Rosalind the shark. He sees me as entertaining and perhaps a little shallow. I see him as proof that passion matters more than profit.
